preview_text:
The model favours Uzbekistan at 45%, with DR Congo trailing at 25%. The statistical forecast sees a nearly 20-percentage-point gap between the two teams, positioning Uzbekistan as the stronger proposition on its numbers alone.

The draw emerges as the modal outcome at 30%, only slightly ahead of an Uzbekistan win. This suggests the match is likely to remain competitive throughout, with a single-goal margin the most probable decisive result. The spread across all three outcomes—while led by Uzbekistan—retains meaningful probability mass in both a DR Congo upset and a stalemate, indicating uncertainty rather than a heavily tilted affair.

The sportsbook consensus diverges notably from the model. Books rate DR Congo at 41%, a 16-percentage-point premium over the model's assessment, and price Uzbekistan at just 30%, a 15-point discount. Polymarket crowd prices align much closer to the model on the Uzbekistan line. This split between traditional and prediction-market operators suggests genuine disagreement on relative team strength; the dashboard makes no claim as to which is correct.
